vitals.date: No 2026 edition — discontinued after 2025 (L'Étape Italy relocated to Piedmont)
vitals.field_size: 2,000+ (2025, riders from 20 countries; exact cap unverified)

fondo_rating.scoring_notes: Migrated from gravel-race-automation. Original score: 77. adventure dimension dropped, road_surface added. Score recalculated: 73.

course_description.character: The 2025 (final) edition started and finished inside Parma's Parco Ducale. It crossed the plains and foothills south of Parma before entering the Emilia-Romagna Apennines around Lesignano de' Bagni, Neviano degli Arduini and Tizzano Val Parma. The defining section climbed through Lagrimone to Schia-Monte Caio and Passo dei Caprioli before descending back toward Parma — long flat urban approaches bookending a concentrated, repeatedly rolling mountain middle.
course_description.signature_challenge: The Lagrimone-Schia/Monte Caio climb (km 56.8-63.9, 7.1 km at roughly 7.3% net average, cresting at 1,228 m before continuing to Passo dei Caprioli at 1,273 m) — the high point of the course, arriving with the finish still 30+ km away.
suffering_zones: Rivalta KOM (km 18.9-22.9, 4.0 km, 165-353 m) — the first real test after the flat urban start
Passo del Crocione KOM (km 33.2-38.6, 5.4 km, finishing at 629 m) — climbed twice, once outbound and again on the return (km 89.4-91.5, 2.1 km, ~7.4% net average)
Lagrimone-Schia/Monte Caio-Passo dei Caprioli — the course's high-mountain block, see signature_challenge

logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotels and stays in Parma, known for food and tourism; book early for event weekend.

history.origin_story: Launched in 2024 as part of the official L'Étape by Tour de France series, run in partnership with A.S.O., the Comune di Parma and the Emilia-Romagna region.

biased_opinion.summary: L'Étape Parma ran for exactly two editions (2024, 2025) before A.S.O. moved L'Étape Italy out of Emilia-Romagna entirely. The official 2026 announcement (27 December 2025) lists two new Piedmont stops — Entracque in May and Mondovì in September — with no Parma date anywhere on the calendar. While it ran, it was a legitimate Tour de France-branded event: closed/controlled roads, real climbing through the Apennines (Lagrimone, Schia, Monte Caio, Passo dei Caprioli), and a genuinely international field (20 countries in 2025). But its own published figures didn't agree with each other even in its final year — the organizer's course guide said 134 km/2,660 m while the municipal roadbook filed for road closures said 133.4 km/3,167 m, a 507 m gap in the same edition's stated climbing.
strengths: Was a real, official Tour de France/A.S.O.-linked event with closed/controlled roads while it ran
Genuine Apennine climbing (Lagrimone, Schia, Monte Caio, Passo dei Caprioli) plus accessible shorter non-competitive distances
Drew a genuinely international field — riders from 20 countries in its final 2025 edition
Substantial city festival, food and Tour-history programming tied to Parma's food-region identity
weaknesses: Discontinued after only two editions (2024-2025) — there is no 2026 Parma date, and none is expected
This event does not currently exist and should not be presented to riders as bookable
Even in its final year, organizer and municipal figures for course elevation disagreed materially (2,660 m vs 3,167 m)
The long course was reportedly modified shortly before race day in 2025, and reported 2,000+ attendance was never a verified field-size figure
biased_opinion.bottom_line: Retired after two editions. If you want the current Italian L'Étape experience, look at L'Étape Piemonte instead — Entracque in May and Mondovì (as Granfondo Alpi del Mare/L'Étape Mondovì) in September 2026.

final_verdict.one_liner: Discontinued — Tour de France-branded gran fondo that ran two editions in Parma's food hills before A.S.O. relocated L'Étape Italy to Piedmont.
final_verdict.should_you_race: No — this event does not exist for 2026 or beyond. If you want the current L'Étape Italy experience, look at L'Étape Piemonte (Entracque, May) or Granfondo Alpi del Mare/L'Étape Mondovì (Mondovì, September).
final_verdict.alternatives: L'Étape Piemonte (Entracque, Cuneo, Italy — race-data/letape-piemonte.json) and Granfondo Alpi del Mare / L'Étape Mondovì by Tour de France (Mondovì, Piedmont — race-data/granfondo-alpi-del-mare.json), the two Piedmont stops that replaced Parma on the 2026 L'Étape Italy calendar.

videos.title: Highlights | 🇮🇹 L'Etape Parma by Tour de France | 2025 🚲 🍕
videos.channel: L'Etape Series by Tour de France
videos.description:
---------

L'Etape Series is a series of amateur cycling events designed to experience the Tour de France Legend. It is the only event where you can race and feel like a Tour de France champion, and where you can enjoy the Tour de France unique atmosphere and passion.

“The Tour de France is the world's greatest cycling race. Each year it is passionately followed by fans around the world so it has naturally given rise to L'Etape by Tour de France series”, says Christian Prudhomme, Tour de France director. 

The series has now expanded to more than 22 international locations including:

🇫🇷 France
🇧🇷 Brazil
🇧🇬 Bulgaria
🇨🇷 Costa Rica
🇨🇿 Czech Republic
🇩🇰 Denmark
🇪🇨 Ecuador
🇺🇸 United States
🇬🇷 Greece
🇦🇪 United Arab Emirates
🇮🇪 Ireland
🇮🇹 Italy
🇲🇽 Mexico
🇵🇱 Poland
🇷🇴 Romania
🇸🇰 Slovakia
🇸🇮 Slovenia
🇹🇼 Taiwan
🇹🇷 Turkey


Among these Tour de France official cycling events is of course L'Étape du Tour. With a peloton of 45 000 riders and 50 nationalities, it is Europe's biggest cycling event. Riding on the same routes and under the same conditions as Tour de France champions: this is the promise that L'Étape du Tour has kept since 1993.

Check out https://www.letapebytourdefrance.com/ for more information.

Join us on:
-Facebook 👉 https://www.facebook.com/letapeseries/
-Instagram 👉 https://www.instagram.com/letapeseries/
-Strava 👉 https://www.strava.com/clubs/145034/

videos.title: Highlights Granfondo L'Etape Parma 2025
videos.description:
L'Étape Parma by Tour de France è l'evento ufficiale del Tour de France per cicloturisti e cicloamatori che si è svolto sabato 3 e domenica 4 maggio 2025 al Parco Ducale di Parma, portando in città l’atmosfera della Grande Boucle.

Il momento clou è stato la Granfondo e la vittoria assoluta nel percorso lungo è andata a Marco Rinaldi (Pigarella Multisport), di Parma, già piazzato nel 2024 nella granfondo di casa. Erano due i possibili percorsi: il lungo di 134 km per 2.660 metri di dislivello e il corto di 81,1 km per 1.095 metri di dislivello. L’evento, co-organizzato da Extragiro e Comune di Parma in collaborazione con A.S.O. e la Regione Emilia-Romagna, ha visto partecipanti da 20 Paesi che hanno formato una lunga fila di ciclisti al momento del via dal parco Ducale che è proseguita per oltre 5 minuti, dopo il via dato con Giammaria Manghi, Coordinatore delle Politiche Sportive della Regione Emilia-Romagna.

Tutti hanno potuto sfidarsi per conquistare le maglie Gialla, A Pois, Verde e Bianca del Tour de France, respirando così ancora di più la leggenda del Tour de France.

eligibility.notes: SUPERSEDES prior 'active' status. The official 2026 L'Étape Italy announcement (27 December 2025) lists only two Piedmont stops — Entracque (May) and Mondovì (September) — with no Parma date. L'Étape Parma ran two editions (2024, 2025; last confirmed 4 May 2025) before being discontinued at this location. Verified 2026-07-24 via codex gpt-5.6-sol foreground research as part of the Roadie Labs editorial wave 3.
